summaries.110470: Issue not reproduced on Samsung Tab A7 (Android 11/12) by triagers, but reporter confirms it persists on GT-I9500 (Android 5.0.1) through version 3.3.0. A related issue (#145872) on a different Samsung device was closed in favor of this one, so the issue remains open; the GT-I9500 is discontinued but the rendering defect via drawLine on older Samsung hardware is kept under investigation.
summaries.181833: Root cause identified: the underlying EditableText in SelectableText creates a text input connection that interferes with right-click gesture handling; on web, the browser's contextmenu event on the hidden textarea triggers a word-selection that propagates as updateEditingValue, bypassing the framework gesture. A contributor (ishaquehassan) proposed and opened PR #183110 overriding onSecondaryTap in _SelectableTextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder; the team clarified that macOS/iOS native behavior (NSTextView) does select a word on right-click so macOS should be preserved. The web path cannot be fixed by disabling the input connection because it is needed for keyboard shortcuts and the native context menu; an engine-level fix to prevent contextmenu events on the hidden textarea is needed but not yet implemented.
summaries.25841: No platform-specific fix or PR has been proposed. Comments document that on iOS, UITextInputMode / UITextInputCurrentInputModeDidChangeNotification can expose the keyboard language, and on Android getCurrentInputMethodSubtype can get it but there is no documented callback for changes. Multiple community workarounds are shared: detecting RTL via regex on typed characters, using intl.Bidi.detectRtlDirectionality, and a third-party package flutter_bidi_text. Issue is stalled with no active work after 6+ years.
summaries.45457: iOS-only bug confirmed across Chinese and Japanese keyboards on master as of late 2022: the floating cursor does not move leftward on initial drag. A commenter traced it to setMarkedText in FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm and found that commenting out the markedTextRange assignment fixes cursor movement but breaks language switching. Status is open with no fix or PR.
summaries.153219: Regression introduced by #150278 (landed in 3.24): the M3 default hint color (onSurfaceVariant) now overrides ThemeData.hintColor because M3 defaults were previously ignored. Workaround confirmed: use InputDecorationTheme.hintStyle instead of ThemeData.hintColor. Team discussed proceeding with the deprecation of ThemeData.hintColor (#116346 / #91772) rather than reverting; no fix PR yet, stalled.
summaries.150326: Engine-level assertion failure in FlKeyEmbedderResponder (lookup_hash_table pressing_records) on Linux Wayland (confirmed on Arch + KDE 6 Wayland and Ubuntu 23.10 Wayland; does not reproduce under XWayland). The engine emits key events with physical/logical key 0 that fail the framework assertion in KeyEventManager.handleKeyData. Stack trace provided. CC'd to @dkwingsmt. Still open as of Flutter 3.29.2 with no fix; workaround is to use XWayland.
summaries.129233: Confirmed on latest stable/master web: when ensureSemantics() is active, the computed font-size in the hidden input element is 13.33px regardless of TextStyle.fontSize. Root impact identified by commenters: iOS Safari auto-zooms inputs with font-size < 16px on focus, causing undesired zoom. The Flutter Inspector shows correct font size; only the DOM attribute is wrong. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.77461: Reproduced on Linux web: after typing through fcitx and committing Chinese text, subsequent Alt+K shortcut presses no longer trigger the Flutter shortcut handler. Linked to #77048 as potentially related. CC'd to @cbracken. Issue labeled framework; no fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.140543: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.16.5 and master 3.18 on both iOS and macOS. The assertion '_debugPreviousLeaders!.isEmpty' is thrown in debug mode when tapping inside CupertinoPicker wrapped by SelectionArea; does not crash in release mode. CC'd to @Renzo-Olivares. No fix or PR.
summaries.160510: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.27.1 and master. When a WidgetSpan containing a RichText/Text widget is selected along with adjacent TextSpan text, the copied clipboard content shows the Unicode object-replacement character (U+FFFC) instead of the text inside the WidgetSpan child. Related to #92506. No fix or PR.
summaries.142763: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.16.9 and master. Root cause: each rebuild of TextField with a changed magnifierConfiguration recreates the TextSelectionOverlay, causing the magnifier to hide and reshow every frame. Assigned to @Hixie who noted partial progress but has been distracted; issue has received stale-assignment bot pings. No PR landed.
summaries.140537: Confirmed reproducible on Windows stable 3.16.5 and master with 3-beolsik Korean IME: the spacebar key press is swallowed and produces no space character in the TextField; the key event is detected but not resolved. Affects Windows only; not reproducible with 2-beolsik. Still open as of 3.24.3 with no fix or PR.
summaries.92589: Confirmed on Linux web (Chrome): pressing Ctrl+Shift to change input language while a TextField is focused throws a KeyUpEvent assertion. A related issue (#89729) was fixed for a different Linux case but this remains. On master the exception no longer blocks keyboard input but still fires. Labeled framework; CC'd to @dkwingsmt. No fix landed.
summaries.99918: Root cause partially identified: iOS Safari ignores the ::selection CSS pseudo-element used to style selection highlights. The selection background color is linked to the caret-color property (WebKit takes caret-color's alpha and uses it for selection background, making it grey when caret-color is transparent). The assigned issue has had no active updates; team notes a larger proposal (#120613) would address it. Workaround: not available without engine changes.
summaries.97269: Windows tablet mode soft keyboard does not dismiss on unfocus. Confirmed to affect Windows 10 and 11. Multiple community workarounds shared: SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide'), FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus() in onTapOutside, RouteObserver to hide on navigation, and pub.dev package flutter_osk. No fix landed; stalled.
summaries.128315: iOS-only issue confirmed on stable and master: Japanese IME suggestion text is auto-inserted when the TextEditingController text is programmatically replaced while a composing region is active. Team suggested setting the full TextEditingValue (including composing region) instead of setting .text and .selection separately. Linked to similar Windows (#126263) and iOS web (#125765) issues. No fix landed.
summaries.89024: Confirmed on stable and master (Android physical device and emulator): dragging a text selection handle in a TextField inside a ListView causes the ListView to scroll in the opposite direction. No root cause or fix identified; no workaround provided. Issue stalled.
summaries.133034: Confirmed on both Android and iOS. Root cause noted: the asynchronous communication between the engine and framework means that when a TextInputFormatter transforms text (e.g., uppercases it), the composing region sent back to the IME is inconsistent, causing dictation to commit and restart after each word. The framework sends a modified editing value that the IME treats as a commit trigger. A synchronous text input update path would likely fix this but is a long-term effort. No short-term fix identified; stalled.
summaries.91603: Confirmed Windows-specific on web: Ctrl+D opens the Chrome bookmark dialog instead of triggering the Flutter shortcut. Ctrl+Q works correctly. Mac equivalent Cmd+D also opens bookmarks. One commenter workaround: calling RawKeyboard.instance.clearKeysPressed() at the right moment. The issue is that the browser intercepts certain keyboard shortcuts before Flutter can handle them; no framework-level fix proposed. Labeled as a new feature request; stalled.
summaries.139352: Confirmed iOS browsers only (Safari and Chrome on iOS); not reproducible on Android Chrome. Long-pressing a TextField causes a phantom keyboard-height white area to appear below the page on subsequent interactions. A commenter noted an additional repro without long-press when inside a bottomSheet on some mobile web devices. Root cause suspected to be incorrect delay in input placement when long-press focuses the field. No fix landed.
summaries.88542: Confirmed on stable 2.2.3 and master: swiping away from a tab containing an autofocused TextField does not remove focus or call deactivate() on the tab widget, while tapping the TabBar correctly triggers deactivate(). deactivate() is called fewer times when swiping than when tapping. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.96173: Root cause: pasting a large run of non-whitespace text (e.g., genome data) causes runaway memory usage because Paragraph rendering tries to lay out one enormous word without line-break opportunities. The issue is distinct from #90063 (general slowness) because the memory blowup is unique to whitespace-free content. Confirmed on macOS and Windows desktops on stable/master. Was mistakenly closed as a duplicate and reopened. Labeled P1 but no fix; stalled with stale-label bot comments.
summaries.120779: Confirmed on macOS stable 3.7.3 and master: VoiceOver reads "Edit text" on focus but does not read the InputDecoration.labelText. Works on Windows with Narrator. CC'd to @cbracken and @chunhtai; no update or PR. Issue is open for contributions.
summaries.158095: Confirmed on Firefox (not Chrome): TextField above a PlatformView (e.g., HtmlElementView) in a dialog receives focus but does not accept keyboard input. Keyboard input is received only after switching browser tabs and returning. Root cause suspected to involve focus management between Flutter's hidden textarea and the platform view iframes in Firefox. Workaround confirmed: wrapping TextField (or the whole dialog) in SelectionArea resolves the issue. PointerInterceptor did not help. Status: open, workaround available.
summaries.135450: Confirmed on stable 3.13.5 and master: when the widget tree is restructured (widget wrapped in a new parent) while a TextField has focus, the keyboard is dismissed even though the FocusNode retains focus. Root cause: EditableText does not re-establish the input connection when a FocusNode with existing focus is assigned. Team noted a GlobalKey should be used when moving a field in the widget tree; marked P3. No fix or PR.
summaries.126962: Root cause: WidgetSpan applies the ambient text scale factor as a scale transform to its child, so any Text widget inside the WidgetSpan is scaled by textScaleFactor twice (once by the transform, once internally by the Text widget). Community workarounds provided: a NonScalingWidgetSpan class that wraps with textScaleFactor: 1/ambient, and discussion of a future WidgetSpan.noAutoScale flag. Team discussion concluded that images in WidgetSpan should not auto-scale since textScaleFactor is only for text. No fix or PR landed.
summaries.154160: Confirmed on Linux and macOS: when using a dead key sequence with a French layout (e.g., [ then e should produce ê), the resulting KeyDownEvent for the combined character carries character: "e" instead of "ê". Does not reproduce on Windows. CC'd to @dkwingsmt. No fix or PR.
summaries.84452: iOS-only. Confirmed on iOS 14.6 and later (14.7.1, 15.4.1). After navigating back from the create-account screen and returning, the AutofillHints.newPassword suggestion prompt appears but no longer auto-fills a strong password. A commenter noted the issue may originate in the engine; another observed that sending the app to background and returning causes the autofill to work. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.134024: Root cause identified: right clicks on web are handled by the browser's native context menu via the underlying hidden textarea/input, and the scroll position of that hidden element is not synced with the Flutter framework's rendered scroll offset. So right-clicking on visible "Line 8" actually right-clicks "Line 3" in the hidden element, causing the selection to jump. A PR was developed but broke for complex multiline values (#135159). This is no longer a blocker for #120613. Status: stalled, no landed fix.
summaries.147538: Confirmed on iOS stable 3.19.6 and master: when a DropdownMenu's top edge aligns exactly with the top of the software keyboard, the dropdown entries panel does not appear (zero visible height). Comments clarify entries do show if the user scrolls up; the panel is actually rendered but clipped by the keyboard boundary. Comparison with Android Compose ExposedDropdownMenuBox shows the native behavior places the list above the keyboard. No workaround or PR; stalled.
